Experiment 0 — Bonzai-OSM smoke test

Completed: 2026-05-04 · Cluster: CINECA Leonardo Booster (1×A100 + free CPU partition) · GPU-h burned: ~1 (vs 12-30 budgeted) · Branch: genai-city-model · Status: GO

What this experiment was

A smoke test. The whole point: confirm the pipeline closes end-to-end — VAE compresses 9-channel rasters → Sketcher (DiT) paints denoised codes in latent space → Inker (autoregressive transformer) reads the painted blueprint via cross-attention and writes vector tokens → eval harness measures everything — without anything blowing up. We are not testing whether the generated cities look good yet; that's Experiments 1-4.

Training loss curves

All three stages trained without divergence. Loss curves are smooth, no NaNs.

VAE — 50 epochs reconstruction (BCE on binary channels + MSE on density + KL regulariser)

Stage A — DiT diffusion (1 epoch in latent space, EDM noise)

Stage B — Inker (1 epoch teacher-forcing, cross-attn to ground-truth raster)

What the synthetic training tiles look like

5,000 synthetic tiles fed the smoke test. Each tile is 2 km × 2 km (512×512 pixels at 4 m/px), encoded as a 9-channel raster. Two density modes: sparse (a few buildings + 2-3 short roads) and dense (8×8 grid roads + diagonal cross-roads + ~64 buildings + landuse polygons + POIs). Each panel below is one channel of the raster; black = mask off, white = mask on; channel 5 (building density, viridis) is continuous.

Sparse-mode example (seed=42)

Dense-mode example (seed=42)

Eval-suite numbers

Metrics ran on the val set (real vs real, self-vs-self) — they verify the eval code runs cleanly on the trained pipeline. Actual sampling from trained models is deferred to Plan 3 / Experiment 1.

Path-1 sanity samples (locally rendered, 0 Leonardo GPU-h)

The smoke eval ran self-vs-self on val ground-truth. To get a real signal, we pulled the trained checkpoints back to a Mac, loaded VAE/DiT/Inker on Apple Silicon, and ran the actual sampling that the original eval skipped. ~1 minute of local CPU/MPS time.

VAE reconstruction — input vs output (after 50 epochs)

The VAE compresses 9 channels × 512² → 4 × 64² and back. Reconstructions are essentially pixel-perfect on synthetic tiles. Green.

DiT (Sketcher) — sampled from pure noise

Each tile below was generated from random Gaussian noise via 25 denoising steps of DPM-Solver++, then decoded through the frozen VAE. After only 1 epoch of training, the DiT is reproducing the synth corpus's grid-and-blocks structure on the road and residential channels. The continuous building-density channel (5) hasn't picked up spatial variation yet (uniform green) and most low-frequency channels (water/green/urban) are correctly empty. Strong green for 1 epoch.

Inker (Stage B) — greedy-sample tokens from a GT raster, decode back to geometry

The Inker reads a ground-truth raster via cross-attention and writes a token stream. After 1 epoch on synth data, decoded geometry is sparse vs the input — the AR head needs much more training to produce dense output. The fact that any structurally-valid GeoJSON comes out (167 tokens → 3 roads / 10 buildings / 2 POIs in the second sample) confirms the constrained-decoding pipeline is wired correctly. Yellow but expected for 1 epoch.

Go signal

  • Loss curves don't diverge: ✅ All three stages trained cleanly, no NaNs.
  • ≥ 90 % well-formed GeoJSON: ✅ Validity rate 1.00 on val token-sequence round-trips.
  • Visual eyeball check on sampled tiles: ⏸ deferred — the eval driver doesn't yet sample from trained checkpoints. Plan 3 will add the load checkpoint → dpmpp_sample → greedy_inker_sample loop.

What's next (Plan 3)

  • Experiment 1: Train ~200M-param Sketcher on real Sweden + Singapore + Sri Lanka tile shards. Track per-channel IoU, FID, conditioning effectiveness. ~80 GPU-h budget.
  • Experiment 2: Train ~300M-param Inker on perfect ground-truth raster input (no domain gap yet). Track building Chamfer, road-graph connectivity, GeoJSON validity. ~120 GPU-h budget.
  • Add the actual sampling loop so we can dump 32 generated tiles per checkpoint for human eyeball QC.

Generated by scripts/render_eval_report.py. Self-contained; all images inlined as base64 PNGs. No external dependencies for viewing.
